item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too goofy for its own good. I like goofiness, don't get me wrong, but this one comes on too strong and leaves me cringing. Maybe if you dialed the incident down a little? Make it a little bit less cartoony? For now I'll have to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "SCP-2854-2" in the incident log confused me for a second because based on the description they would both be classified as -1. Maybe a better way to distinguish the two entities would be to make them -1A and -1B?

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: This is fine. I agree with this, yes I do.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Edits made as discussed below.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block
  • Entities with several pairs of wings and heads similar to depictions in Christian iconography

This line seems to be describing a typical Biblical angel, and a very well rated version of one of those exists on the site. Crosslinking would strengthen the idea that these are angels.

  • Entities with several pairs of wings and heads similar to depictions in Christian iconography
  • Varying entities of different sizes, shapes, and anomalous ability with iconography similar to depictions of the Christian underworld.

This next line describes typical demons, and there are a lot more examples of those on the site. I've chosen 1844, but there are a lot that could work for this.

  • Varying entities of different sizes, shapes, and anomalous ability with iconography similar to depictions of the Christian underworld.

Police Officers and UIU agents arrive approximately fifteen minutes later and begin to form a perimeter around the establishment. Customers have begun to vacate the store at this time, running past the UIU agents and to their cars, driving off. One of the UIU agents appears to be speaking into his radio, before stepping forward from his vehicle and grabbing a megaphone from the passenger side seat.

Standard "link GoI to Hub" page proposal for this line.

Police Officers and UIU agents arrive approximately fifteen minutes later and begin to form a perimeter around the establishment. Customers have begun to vacate the store at this time, running past the UIU agents and to their cars, driving off. One of the UIU agents appears to be speaking into his radio, before stepping forward from his vehicle and grabbing a megaphone from the passenger side seat.

Closing Statement: After the Foundation arrive on the scene, the police officers break perimeter and drive off. After cordoning the area, Foundation personnel coordinate with UIU agents to distribute cover stories about paranormal activity in the affected establishment. UIU agents catalog the entities and Foundation personnel capture them in Type III Spectral Mutagen Allocators.

This final line makes not of some sort of Ghostbusters-esque ghost trap. Something very similar to this already exists on the wiki in the form of a Prometheus Labs Grant Request, and a crosslink could be made.

Closing Statement: After the Foundation arrive on the scene, the police officers break perimeter and drive off. After cordoning the area, Foundation personnel coordinate with UIU agents to distribute cover stories about paranormal activity in the affected establishment. UIU agents catalog the entities and Foundation personnel capture them in Type III Spectral Mutagen Allocators.

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy crap I reviewed this in draft form and came up with the name Babylon Whore's Corner Store and it's been months and I hadn't even known it had been posted oh my

Take all of my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are we really going for the UIUseless thing?

Edit: +1, btw. Quite entertaining to read, especially if u have like actually shitty customers, this pair is actually nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or maybe we could get a record of humans entering by accident and buying things (Or trying, since they don't have the right currency)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. It was supposed to be an average of 5 minutes. I must have forgot to put that in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mention items restocking themselves after 5 minutes, but you also mention the shortest time being a few seconds. Was this supposed to be an average of 5 minutes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. I never thought to make it a -j. However I could see it working like one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems like a -j scp, and in that regard could have been played with better social commentary for Walmart. Welcome to Walmart! I'm sorry we don't have an essence of the damned section, but I'll be sure to bring it up to our store manager. Look, we just take cash and credit, no lost souls. Would you like to apply for our membership club? At least they didn't have screaming children.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoting on behalf of the UIU. Its becasue of stuff like this that the term "UIUseless" exists. The UIU are much more competent then running away as the foundation arrive. At least have then have a part in containment or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am a loud supporter for gooffy main articles, and this is short, simple and funny. However, I feel like this could have been fleshed out more, and my expectations were higher than this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the wal-mart store employees don't feel very realistic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd love to see an expanded log of anomalous items recovered from Babylons. Also, what happens if someone attempts to remove an item without paying? Or tries to pay with earthly cash? Or attempts to counterfeit the demon currency?

+1 for what it is, +10 for the possibilities!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Babylon Whore's Corner Store, when you want the very Beast.

This is entertaining. I second psul; getting more of what the 2854-1 instances think of the bizarre new shopping environment would be good. Letting UIU be effective would be a nice touch.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While this has improved since the draft I read, I don't think that you're taking it far enough. If the gag is "horrific demons want to shop just like us", then I would have preferred to spend more time with them, and see their reactions to things, to get an idea of their "through the looking glass" world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm with this. Not only do they do nothing, it's really strange to cut from dialogue-recording security footage to them outside talking to eachother for just a few sentences.

I'd kind of prefer either they do something more effective/proactive, or cut their dialogue and just have them in the summary.

+1 anyway, this was silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked this as a draft, still like it now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really glad to have seen this take form, and I'm happy to have helped you with it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, good point. I guess I should have used their last names instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding the UIU thing, especially if the agents in questions are Darnell Christman and Quinn MacAllister. You're using their first names, not their last.

Also, I'm not sure what a pair of midwestern agents would be doing in North Carolina. Just sayin'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's indeed improved from what I read. However, I don't really fancy UIU's inaction here. Even if we factor in UIUseless, I think they can at least try to control the panic of the situation. Maybe they might not be able to confront the demons, they can make sure no one gets hurt.

And really, the situation is not that hard since the demons don't mean harm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks bud, I really liked this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This draft took me at least three weeks in the making.

My thanks to everyone that looked over this draft, there are too many to list. Westrin, BananaRepublic, tretter, et al.

Hope you enjoy it.
